title: arthrocnemum fruticosum
intro: Arthrocnemum fruticosum, also known as glasswort or sea purslane, is a perennial halophytic shrub that grows in salt marshes and other coastal areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Arthrocnemum fruticosum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and act as a natural diuretic. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
